content.question: What is the causal direction and magnitude of the school-closure/population-decline relationship specifically in Hungarian villages? Does closure trigger out-migration, does out-migration necessitate closure, or do both respond to unobserved economic shocks?
content.reduction_path: Commission a Hungary-specific difference-in-differences or matching study comparing treated (closed) and control (surviving) villages in comparable micro-regions from 2007 onward, controlling for baseline demographic trends, employment, and local economic conditions; conduct structured interviews with households in closed-school villages to assess whether school loss influenced migration decisions versus post-hoc rationalization.
